pi_items.source: Verified via install_gvsig.sh which downloads and extracts the Natural Earth dataset to this location.
Verified via task instructions and setup_task.sh which prepares this directory for the output.
Verified via public geographical data (Brazil's true area is 8,515,767 km²). Code execution confirmed that reprojecting the downloaded shapefile's Brazil geometry to ESRI:54034 yields ~8,508,160 km².

Verified via public geographical data (Greenland's true area is 2,166,086 km²). Code execution confirmed that reprojecting the downloaded shapefile's Greenland geometry to ESRI:54034 yields ~2,207,084 km².

Verified by calculating shapefile area errors vs true areas (Brazil 0.1% error, Greenland 1.9% error); 15% is an appropriate threshold to accommodate 1:110m generalization while catching massive projection errors (e.g. Mercator).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real-world 'Natural Earth' 1:110m country vectors. The shapefile is downloaded to /home/ga/gvsig_data/countries/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp. The task requires calculating true country areas in square kilometers using an equal-area projection. Independent code execution confirms that when reprojected to an equal-area projection like EPSG:54034 (or ESRI:54034), the shapefile yields approximately 8,508,160 km² for Brazil (true area ~8,515,767) and 2,207,084 km² for Greenland (true area ~2,166,086). The 15% tolerance is appropriate and sufficient to absorb the shapefile's generalization error while definitively distinguishing from non-equal-area distortions.
